To find the probability that a student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A we define the difference D as X_B minus X_A. Since both variables are normally distributed D is also normal with mean 75 minus 70 which equals 5 and variance 12 squared plus 10 squared which equals 244. The standard deviation of D is the square root of 244 or approximately 15.62. We calculate the Z score as 0 minus 5 divided by 15.62 which gives roughly -0.32. The probability that D is greater than 0 corresponds to the area to the right of this Z score which is approximately 0.6255.

For the second part we must find the time t where the exponential growth of Group A equals the logistic growth of Group B. The equation is 50 times e to the power of 0.02t equals 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of -0.15 times t minus 5. This mathematical process requires numerical methods to solve for t because the equation mixes exponential and logistic terms in a complex way. There is nothing simple about finding the exact intersection point without iterative calculation. The force of the differing growth rates means they will cross at a specific moment. An explanation of the solution shows that at t equals approximately 6.8 the performance levels converge.
